ops.text: RESULTS E43 — CLOSED, VERIFY-confirmed (independent own-code macro-F1; encoder_human collapse confirmed real, not a scoring artifact). VERDICT: a cheap local encoder PARTIALLY distills the judge but CANNOT match the expensive LLM at human-agreement — bottleneck is human-label data, not architecture. EXP A (distill the judge, eval_a frozen 250): encoder_judge accuracy 0.657 (per-seed 0.644/0.664/0.664, sd 0.014) — beats majority 0.408 and TF-IDF 0.468 (encoder−tfidf +0.051, session-clustered CI [+0.024, +0.102] excludes 0); macro-F1 only 0.258 because the head emits just 5 of 11 classes. So distillation WORKS on the dominant categories (accuracy) but not the tail. EXP B (match the human, eval_b auditor-gold 191): encoder_human macro-F1 0.054 — a GENUINE data-starved FAILURE (all 3 seeds collapse to ~88% operational_directive; only 174 human train rows for an 11-way head), nowhere near the real incumbent (stored triage_category vs human gold) macro-F1 0.589 (recovers E30.3's ~0.59). So NO — a cheap encoder can't yet replace the expensive judge at agreeing with the human; the lever is more human labels (the pipeline has ~174), not a bigger model. eval_b label-noise caveat (191 rows / 134 unique texts; 41 dup-id groups, 15 with conflicting human gold — caps achievable accuracy). Answers the user's "small specific model for categorizing the kind of steer": it can cheaply approximate the JUDGE's frequent categories, but replacing the LLM at the HUMAN-quality task is data-bound. Artifacts: E43/out/{results.json, report.md, preds/}, verify2/, data_manifest.json.
